docs.title: Johan Hallberg Szabadváry
docs.text:
Industrial PhD Student in Computational Mathematics Stockholm University and Jönköping University

I am a mathematician and data scientist bridging the gap between rigorous theory and industrial application.

My research focuses on Conformal Prediction, Time-Series Forecasting, and Reliable Machine Learning. I divide my time between academic research at Stockholm University and applying these methods in production at Jönköping Energi and Siemens Energy.

Prior to my career in mathematics, I worked professionally in the performing arts as an actor and musician. I remain active in the local music scene on the Swedish West Coast.

docs.title: Recent Highlights
docs.text:
• Performance: Christmas concert in Ljungskile, Dec 20.
  • Service: Appointed as Publications Chair for COPA 2026.
  • Software Launch: Released beta-kde, a new library for boundary-corrected density estimation.
  • Paper Accepted: "Beyond conformal predictors: Adaptive Conformal Inference with confidence predictors" published in Pattern Recognition.

View Full CV View Research

docs.title: Education
docs.text:
PhD in Computational Mathematics Stockholm University (2022 – Present)

MSc in Mathematics Stockholm University & KTH Royal Institute of Technology (2016 – 2022)

  • Interests: Optimization, mathematical modelling, dynamical systems, optimal control, numerical analysis, and complex analysis.

docs.title: Experience
docs.text:
Industrial PhD Student Stockholm University (2023 – Present)

  • Focus: Reliable machine learning, specifically through conformal prediction.

  • Applications: Short-term electricity demand forecasting for decision-making under uncertainty and predictive maintenance in industrial manufacturing.

  • Partners: Jönköping Energy and Siemens Energy.

Data Scientist Algorithma (2022 – Present)

  • Algorithma is an AI and Data Science consultancy firm.
    • Key Skills: AI, Machine Learning, Python, Consulting.

Master Thesis Student Ericsson Research (2022)

  • Key Skills: Python, Machine Learning, Mathematical Analysis, Probability Theory, Scikit-learn, Kubernetes, Julia.

Teaching Assistant Stockholm University (2018 – 2022)

  • Led tutorials and computer labs in Python, Matlab, and Mathematica.

docs.title: Academic Service & Leadership
docs.text:
• Publications Chair: Symposium on Conformal and Probabilistic Prediction with Applications (COPA 2026)
  • Guest Lecturer: Royal Holloway, University of London (2025)

docs.title: Other Experience
docs.text: Performing Arts (Actor & Musician) Various Productions * worked professionally in musical theatre and stage productions prior to academic career. * Continues to perform locally in Western Sweden.

docs.title: Research
docs.text: My research lies at the intersection of computational mathematics and reliable machine learning, with a specific focus on Conformal Prediction, decision-making under uncertainty, and non-parametric statistics.

docs.title: Peer-Reviewed Publications
docs.text:
• Classification with Reject Option: Distribution-free Error Guarantees via Conformal Prediction J. Hallberg Szabadváry, T. Löfström, U. Johansson, C. Sönströd, E. Ahlberg, L. Carlsson Machine Learning with Applications (2025) [ DOI ]

  • Beyond Conformal Predictors: Adaptive Conformal Inference with Confidence Predictors J. Hallberg Szabadváry, T. Löfström Pattern Recognition (2025) [ DOI ]

  • Conformal Predictive Decision Making: A Comparative Study with Bayesian and Point-Predictive Methods S. Lanngren, M. Toremark, J. Hallberg Szabadváry Proceedings of Machine Learning Research, Vol 266 (2025)

  • online-cp: a Python Package for Online Conformal Prediction J. Hallberg Szabadváry, T. Löfström, R. Matela Proceedings of Machine Learning Research, Vol 266 (2025) [ Code ]

  • Conformal LLM Multi-label Text Classification with Binary Relevance Approach V. Ornbratt, J. Hallberg Szabadváry Proceedings of Machine Learning Research, Vol 266 (2025)

  • Adaptive Conformal Inference for Multi-Step Ahead Time-Series Forecasting Online J. Hallberg Szabadváry Proceedings of the 13th Symposium on Conformal and Probabilistic Prediction with Applications (COPA 2024) [ PDF ]

  • Evaluation of conformal-based probabilistic forecasting methods for short-term wind speed forecasting S. Althoff, J. Hallberg Szabadváry, J. Anderson, L. Carlsson Proceedings of COPA 2023, PMLR 204:100-115

  • On Qualitative Analysis of a Discrete Time SIR Model J. Hallberg Szabadváry, Y. Zhou Chaos, Solitons & Fractals: X (2021) [ DOI ]

docs.text:
• beta-kde (2025) A Scikit-learn compatible library for boundary-corrected kernel density estimation. [ Documentation | GitHub ]

  • online-cp (2025) A comprehensive Python package for Online Conformal Prediction. [ GitHub ]

docs.title: Upcoming Performance: Christmas Concert in Ljungskile
docs.text:
I am happy to announce the annual Christmas concert at Cirkuskvarnen in Ljungskile will take place this year as well

Date: December 20, 2025

Location: Cirkuskvarnen, Buddeberg 113, 451 91, Uddevalla

Time: 18:30

I will be performing alongside Kinga Szabadváry, Julia Svensson, Elinor Josefsson, and Johanna Abrahamsson in a Christmas concert for the whole family.

This serves as a nice break from my current work on Conformal Prediction.

Download Concert Poster
